import torch
import json
import argparse
import os
from model import SAKT

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument('--data_path', required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--checkpoint', required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--output', required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    # Load test data
    test_data = torch.load(os.path.join(args.data_path, 'test.pt'))
    num_skills = test_data['num_skills']
    max_seq_len = test_data['skill_ids'].shape[1]
    
    device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
    
    model = SAKT(num_skills=num_skills, max_seq_len=max_seq_len, d_model=128, n_heads=8, n_layers=4, dropout=0.2)
    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(args.checkpoint, map_location=device))
    model = model.to(device)
    model.eval()
    
    skill_ids = test_data['skill_ids'].to(device)
    corrects = test_data['corrects'].to(device)
    lengths = test_data['lengths'].to(device)
    
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits = model(skill_ids, corrects, lengths)
        pred_logits = logits[:, :-1]
        probs = torch.sigmoid(pred_logits)
    
    T = corrects.shape[1]
    mask = torch.arange(T - 1, device=device).unsqueeze(0) < (lengths.unsqueeze(1) - 1)
    predictions = probs[mask].cpu().tolist()
    
    with open(args.output, 'w') as f:
        json.dump({'predictions': predictions}, f)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
